text.title: Eu Sovereign Llm Deployment Bundle Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Eu Sovereign Llm Deployment Bundle must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: All inference traffic, including telemetry, terminates inside the declared jurisdiction. Cross-border egress (even for monitoring SaaS) requires an explicit DPA + processor record.
rule.rationale: any cross-border path defeats sovereignty claims regardless of encryption.

rule.statement: Document the model lineage: base weights provenance, fine-tune dataset DPIA, and any RLHF labels — all stored in-jurisdiction and accessible to the auditor.

rationale.source: EU AI Act 2024 transparency obligations for foundation-model deployments

rule.rationale: EU AI Act 2024 transparency obligations for foundation-model deployments.

rule.statement: Run a daily eval pinned to the local model build hash; rollback triggers if regression vs control set exceeds the documented threshold.
rule.rationale: drift in self-hosted stacks is silent without an eval gate.

rule.statement: Network egress allowlist must be explicit; default-deny on outbound. Sidecar inspection must be sovereign too.

rationale.source: Zero-trust networking + Schrems II compliance practice

rule.rationale: Zero-trust networking + Schrems II compliance practice.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Multi-model gateway migration: lock-in to portability (2 months) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
